description:
GOAL: Widen the existing `HostCtx` (passed as the trampoline's second
arg to every dispatched fn) into a `Process` struct that owns the heap
+ builders + jit-ctx. Drop the thread_local!s in ir_codegen.rs and
jit.rs in favor of the already-threaded ctx pointer. Sets up multi-
thread scheduling (near-term per user, 2026-05-11), closes the
re-entrance footgun, and makes thread-safety visible in FFI signatures.

## Decisions captured (user, 2026-05-11)

- De-TLS strategy: OPTION 3a — pass `*mut Process` as a hidden first
  argument to every runtime FFI fn. NOT option 3c (TLS-held ptr).
  Reasoning: multi-thread is near-term; explicit ctx (i) makes
  per-process scope visible at every call site, (ii) closes the
  re-entrance hole, (iii) enables `send(receiver_pid, msg)` to pass
  both contexts without back-channel, (iv) is faster than TLS access
  on hot path.

## What changes is SMALLER than originally framed

The trampoline (src/ir_codegen.rs:124-154) ALREADY threads a per-run
mutable context as the second arg of every dispatched fn:

```rust
pub struct HostCtx { pub halt_value: i64 }                  // line 158
let mut host_ctx = HostCtx { halt_value: 0 };               // line 132
cur = f(cur, &mut host_ctx as *mut HostCtx as *mut u8);     // line 151
```

The dispatched fn FFI signature is already
`extern "C" fn(*mut u8, *mut u8) -> *mut u8` where slot 2 is the ctx.

So this ticket is NOT "introduce ctx threading"; it's "widen `HostCtx`
into `Process`, drop the TLS reach-arounds in the inner runtime fns,
have them take ctx as a new arg, and update Cranelift call sites to
pass ctx through".

## Deliverables

### 1. Widen HostCtx -> Process

```rust
pub struct Process {
    pub heap: Heap,                       // was thread_local! HEAP
    pub map_builder: Option<Vec<(u64, u64)>>,  // was thread_local! MAP_BUILDER
    pub halt_value: i64,                  // retain from HostCtx
    // .19.1 will add: pid, next_frame, mailbox, state
    // .11.31 will add: gc threshold flag (or fold into heap)
}
```

`fz_runtime` JIT context (jit.rs:175 JIT_CTX) is in a different file
and serves a different purpose (interp-bridge for legacy JIT path);
audit whether it remains TLS-only or also moves to Process. Resolve
in implementation. NOTE: jit.rs may be on its way out as the legacy
JIT path is replaced by CPS codegen; if jit.rs is dead, dropping
JIT_CTX TLS is just deletion.

### 2. Runtime fn signature migration

Every `fz_*` extern "C" fn that reaches `HEAP.with(...)` or
`MAP_BUILDER.with(...)` today gets a new first arg `ctx: *mut Process`.
Internal body:

```rust
extern "C" fn fz_alloc_struct(ctx: *mut Process, schema_id: u32) -> *mut u8 {
    let process = unsafe { &mut *ctx };
    process.heap.alloc_struct(schema_id) as *mut u8
}
```

Audit list (greppable; line numbers post-.11.25 cleanup):

- fz_print_value (line ~166)
- fz_render_value_parts (~213) — uses HEAP for schema lookup
- fz_alloc_struct (~471)
- fz_map_new / fz_map_finish / fz_map_push (~515-567)
- fz_alloc_bitstring (~752)
- fz_alloc_list_cons / fz_alloc_list_nil (TBD — grep)
- fz_alloc_closure (~1329)
- fz_alloc_float (~954, ~1003, ~1026)
- fz_alloc_struct sites inside bitstring/reader/result helpers
  (~830, ~894, ~969, ~978)
- fz_alloc_frame — already takes (schema_id, total_size); add ctx
- Any other HEAP.with(...) site (search for substring; ~20 total)

### 3. Cranelift call-site changes (src/ir_codegen.rs)

Each emit-Prim / emit-Term lowering that emits an FFI Call instruction:

- Signature builder: prepend `AbiParam::new(types::I64)` for ctx ptr.
- Call site: load ctx from the current block's ctx-param (block-param
  threading, RECOMMENDED for v1) and pass as first arg.
- Entry block: the JIT'd fn already takes (cur: i64, ctx: i64) per
  trampoline ABI. Bind the second entry param as the ctx for the rest
  of the fn; thread through to children via block-params or reload
  from frame.

DECISION at impl time: block-param threading vs frame-slot stash.
RECOMMENDED: block-param. Simpler; ctx is hot in registers; perf
follow-up later if profile shows reg pressure.

### 4. Trampoline change (src/ir_codegen.rs:124-154)

```rust
pub fn run(&self, fn_id: FnId, process: &mut Process) -> i64 {
    // ... existing frame alloc ...
    let ctx = process as *mut Process;
    while !cur.is_null() {
        // ... existing dispatch ...
        cur = f(cur, ctx as *mut u8);
    }
    process.halt_value
}
```

Callers of `run()` construct the Process. For the single-process v1
(pre-.19.1), construct a default Process with `Heap::new(...)` and run.

### 5. TLS removal

Delete:
- `HEAP` thread_local! (line ~422)
- `MAP_BUILDER` thread_local! (line ~487)
- `JIT_CTX` thread_local! in jit.rs (if jit.rs still alive)

TLS that STAYS (test-only, document with a comment):
- TEST_CAPTURE (line ~358)
- IR_TEXT_RECORD (~609)

### 6. fz_runtime atom table (intern/name_of)

DO NOT migrate. Per fz-ul4.19.6, atom table is global shared singleton.
Stays as-is.

## Tests (TDD order)

1. Construct a `Process`, run a trivial fn (`fn main(), do: 42`),
   assert halt_value == 42. (Replaces existing CompiledModule.run-with-
   default-heap path.)
2. Two `Process` instances on the same OS thread, each running a small
   map-building program with disjoint keys, assert both finish with
   correct values. PRE-MIGRATION this would corrupt because MAP_BUILDER
   is shared TLS.
3. Two Processes alternately running short steps — confirms ctx
   propagation correctness even with interleaving (manual scheduling
   via direct .run() calls; full Scheduler waits for .19.1).
4. Existing SAMPLE-based tests still pass (regression).
5. Codegen unit test: every emitted FFI Call has ctx in slot 0.
   (Implement as IR inspection or by running with a stub ctx that
   sets a sentinel byte and asserting fns observed it.)

## Risks

- Large mechanical diff (every FFI site + every emit site). Mitigation:
  land in a single commit; do not leave half-migrated state.
- Cranelift reg pressure: extra block-param. Mitigation: measure on
  count(100000, 0) post-change; expect noise-level diff.
- `fz_runtime`'s JIT_CTX (jit.rs:175): audit whether jit.rs is dead
  code post-CPS-cutover. If so, just delete it.

## Out of scope

- Scheduler / spawn / pid (.19.1).
- Multi-thread synchronization on Heap flags or atom table.
- Splitting Heap into per-arena (.19.5).
- GC safepoints / root walking (.11.31).

## References

- src/ir_codegen.rs:124-154 (trampoline; ctx already threaded)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:158-160 (HostCtx — becomes Process)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:422-424 (HEAP TLS — delete)
- src/ir_codegen.rs:487-488 (MAP_BUILDER TLS — delete)
- src/jit.rs:175 (JIT_CTX TLS — audit/delete)
- src/heap.rs:73-96 (Heap struct — becomes Process.heap)

## Acceptance

- All TLS runtime state removed (test TLS retained with comment).
- `Process` struct in scope; `run()` takes `&mut Process`.
- Two-Process same-thread map test passes.
- Full test suite green.
- One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.32.
title: Per-Process runtime context: thread ctx ptr through FFI (de-TLS)
